parts._note: Represents Raspberry Pi 3B GPIO. Pin mapping: 0=GPIO17, 1=GPIO27, 2=GPIO22, 3=GPIO23, 5=GPIO12(PWM0), 6=GPIO13(PWM1)
L298N dual H-bridge motor driver. 6V motor supply from 4xAA battery pack. Drives both DC motors.
Left drive motor. 6V brushed DC with encoder. TT gearmotor.
Right drive motor. 6V brushed DC with encoder. TT gearmotor.
Motor power supply: 4xAA NiMH rechargeable (4 x 1.2V = 4.8V, or 4x alkaline = 6V). Separate from Pi power to avoid motor noise on logic rail.
Pi power supply: 5V 2.5A USB power bank. Must supply at least 2A for stable Pi 3B + camera operation. Connected via micro-USB, NOT through GPIO 5V pin in production.
Status LED: solid = ready, blinking = streaming, off = error. Connected to GPIO 18 (Pi pin 7).
Current limiting resistor for status LED. 220 ohm. (3.3V - 2V) / 220 = 5.9mA, safe for GPIO.
HC-SR04 ECHO voltage divider (upper). 1kΩ. Divides 5V ECHO signal down to 3.3V safe for Pi GPIO 25. Part of 1k/2k divider: 5V × 2k/(1k+2k) = 3.33V.
HC-SR04 ECHO voltage divider (lower). 2kΩ. Goes to GND. Combined with R1=1k gives 3.33V output from 5V ECHO, safe for Pi 3.3V GPIO.

_wiring_notes.board: Raspberry Pi 3 Model B (represented as Arduino Uno in diagram tool)

gpio_pin_mapping.Diagram Pin 0: Pi GPIO 17 → L298N IN1 (Left Motor Direction A)
gpio_pin_mapping.Diagram Pin 1: Pi GPIO 27 → L298N IN2 (Left Motor Direction B)
gpio_pin_mapping.Diagram Pin 2: Pi GPIO 22 → L298N IN3 (Right Motor Direction A)
gpio_pin_mapping.Diagram Pin 3: Pi GPIO 23 → L298N IN4 (Right Motor Direction B)
gpio_pin_mapping.Diagram Pin 5: Pi GPIO 12 → L298N ENA (Left Motor PWM Speed)
gpio_pin_mapping.Diagram Pin 6: Pi GPIO 13 → L298N ENB (Right Motor PWM Speed)

HC-SR04 Ultrasonic.TRIG: Pi GPIO 24 (output, 3.3V is enough to trigger)
HC-SR04 Ultrasonic.ECHO: Pi GPIO 25 via voltage divider (1kΩ + 2kΩ to GND). ECHO outputs 5V, Pi GPIO max is 3.3V. Divider: 5V × 2k/(1k+2k) = 3.33V.

MPU-6050 IMU.VCC: Pi 3.3V (MPU-6050 has onboard regulator, but 3.3V is cleaner)
MPU-6050 IMU.SDA: Pi GPIO 2 (I2C SDA, pin 3 on header)
MPU-6050 IMU.SCL: Pi GPIO 3 (I2C SCL, pin 5 on header)
MPU-6050 IMU.INT: Pi GPIO 4 (optional interrupt, not required initially)
MPU-6050 IMU.mounting: Center of chassis, flat, aligned with direction of travel

ArduCam IMX708 Camera.connection: CSI ribbon cable to Pi camera port
ArduCam IMX708 Camera.power: Powered by Pi via CSI connector

motor_supply.source: 4× AA NiMH rechargeable batteries in series holder
motor_supply.notes: L298N drops ~2V, so motors see ~2.8-4V. Adequate for TT gearmotors. If more power needed, upgrade to 2S LiPo (7.4V).

logic_supply.notes: MUST be separate from motor power. Motor noise will crash the Pi if shared. Power bank must supply 2.5A continuous.

common_ground.critical: L298N GND and Pi GND MUST be connected together. Without common ground, logic signals cannot reference the same voltage level and motors will not respond to commands.

motor_control_logic.speed: ENA and ENB control speed via PWM (0-100% duty cycle). Start at 50% and tune.

safety.voltage_divider: REQUIRED on HC-SR04 ECHO pin. 5V into Pi GPIO will permanently damage the pin.
safety.flyback_diodes: L298N has built-in flyback diodes. No external diodes needed.
safety.decoupling_caps: Add 100nF ceramic capacitor across each motor terminal to suppress EMI noise. Solder directly to motor.
safety.fuse: Optional: 3A blade fuse on motor battery positive lead.
